item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I still don't understand why McIntosh initially thinks that it is Roget and not Davis, or why HM's Admiralty deemed it necessary for the HMS Davis to false flag in order to pass the checkpoint. I guess maybe Davis doesn't have access?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Andy Toxie, I don't even.

I had to upvote for "HMS Not-Roget", though "They were a good crew, rich with protein" was equally funny. I don't recall being a huge fan of 2324, but this is amusing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: While I love the potential here, I'm downvoting for the Foundation being terribly incompetent, and then getting extraordinarily lucky - nothing in the writing was engaging enough to get over that. I was very much hoping to upvote the bee navy, and look forward to another chance to do so.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is literally the precise reason I don't headcanon the Foundation hiring two people from the same family. I mean, some of the stories can be cool, but they belong not in my head artillery.

Neutral voted, may change my mind later.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I thought Clef at least was a codename acquired after he changed allegiances. At any rate, I'm not sure if "they did it like that in the beginning" is actually ever a good reason to do anything now. Don't get me wrong, it's not a deal breaker here. I just wanted to point out that it stands out in a not good way for me.

I am more concerned with my first question anyway.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: If we have peopled named Clef, Gears, and Lament, I can believe Toxie.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I am a little confused as how the HMS Donald Davis was able to impersonate Dr. Roget, or why it was even deemed necessary by HM's admiralty (wouldn't Davis be sufficient cover?).

Also Dr. Toxie the Toxicologist reminds me of a podiatrist my mother saw when she broke a toe: Dr. Foote (I also apparently once had a Dr. Kidd as my primary pediatrician). I don't know if that works as well in fiction, due to conservation of WTF or something.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Something amusing I wrote in math class. Hope you like it.
